description_long: This is a Russian police car, specifically a Lada Samara, painted primarily in white with a blue horizontal stripe running along the sides. The vehicle features Cyrillic text, including 'МИЛИЦИЯ' (Militsiya, meaning 'police') and 'МОСКВА' (Moscow), as well as a red and blue city emblem on the front doors. A roof-mounted light bar with red and blue emergency lights is prominently displayed, and the car has four doors, black side mirrors, and standard steel wheels. The windows are tinted dark, and the car's body shows some minor weathering and dirt, suggesting urban use. The front and rear views reveal standard headlights, taillights, and a rear trunk, but no visible license plate.
description: A white Lada Samara police car with blue stripes, Russian police markings, and a red-blue light bar on the roof.
description_view_0: Side view showing the blue stripe, Cyrillic police markings, Moscow emblem, and the full length of the car.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the windshield, headlights, and the red-blue emergency light bar on the roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with similar blue stripe, police markings, and emblem, showing the passenger side details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back window, taillights, and absence of a visible license plate.
attribute_police_markings: The car has blue Cyrillic text 'МИЛИЦИЯ' (police) on the front doors and 'МОСКВА' (Moscow) with additional smaller text on the rear doors. A blue horizontal stripe runs along both sides of the vehicle. The Moscow city emblem is present on the front doors below the text.
attribute_emergency_lights: A roof-mounted light bar with red and blue rectangular emergency lights is centered on the roof, visible from all sides.
attribute_police_insignia: A red and blue Moscow city emblem is visible on both front doors, serving as a police insignia.
